page: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor running at 2.0 GHz. The configuration includes 2 shared vCPUs mapped to 2 physical cores with 1 thread per core. Memory capacity is limited to 2.0 GB, yielding 1.0 GB of RAM per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 30 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core execution is highly efficient, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression all ranking in the top tier (top 10%). However, multi-core and multi-threaded operations show significant degradation, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression and the overall PassMark CPU score falling into the weak category (bottom 25%). Memory performance is a notable bottleneck; the PassMark memory score is poor (bottom 10%), although cached small-block read bandwidth remains average at 418.11 GB/sec.

This server offers a low-cost entry point suitable for lightweight workloads, but users face clear tradeoffs. The shared CPU allocation and low memory-to-core ratio limit its utility for memory-intensive applications, as reflected in the weak database operations and Redis SET benchmarks. Static web serving throughput and small-model LLM inference speeds are average. This instance is best suited for basic web serving, single-threaded utility scripts, lightweight development environments, and low-traffic applications that do not require sustained multi-core processing or large memory footprints.
description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual server featuring 2 shared vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 30 GB of local SSD storage. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance in stress-ng and compression benchmarks. However, it faces performance trade-offs, with weak multi-threaded decompression, a weak PassMark CPU score, and a poor PassMark memory score. The shared CPU allocation and low memory density make it a budget-friendly option for workloads with low resource demands. It is best suited for basic web serving, lightweight utility scripts, and development environments that do not require high memory capacity or sustained multi-core execution.
og_description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-2GB is an entry-level KVM virtual server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and 30 GB SSD storage, offering strong single-core performance alongside weak multi-core and memory metrics.
meta_description: UpCloud STARTER-2xCPU-2GB is a budget KVM server with 2 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and 30 GB SSD storage, suitable for basic workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU instance featuring strong single-core processing paired with basic memory and local SSD storage for lightweight workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 2 shared vCPUs and x86_64 architecture
2.0 GB RAM and 30 GB of bundled local SSD storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and compression performance
Weak multi-core execution and poor memory benchmark scores
Suited for basic web serving, lightweight scripts, and testing environments
